title: Implement X-Frame-Options Header to Prevent Clickjacking
description:
The server was not returning an `X-Frame-Options` header, leaving the application vulnerable to clickjacking attacks where a malicious site could embed DataTools Pro pages in an iframe.

## Requirements

- Add `X-Frame-Options: DENY` header to all HTTP responses.
- Implement via Laravel middleware for centralized control (not per-page).
- Design must allow future flexibility to permit iframe embedding within Salesforce when needed.
acceptance_criteria: X-Frame-Options header is set to DENY on all HTTP responses
Implementation uses Laravel middleware for centralized control
No pages can be embedded in iframes by external sites
The middleware can be updated in one place when Salesforce iframe embedding is needed
middleware_changes: Laravel middleware added to set X-Frame-Options: DENY on all HTTP responses. Centralized implementation allows single-point modification when Salesforce iframe embedding is needed.
implementation_notes: Implemented via Laravel middleware to ensure the header is applied to all requests centrally. Product owner emphasized the need for centralized control rather than per-page implementation, anticipating future need to allow Salesforce iframe embedding.
comment_summary: Product owner wanted centralized implementation (not per-page) with future flexibility for Salesforce iframe embedding. Developer implemented via Laravel middleware and confirmed the centralized approach. Header verified in response headers.
